role: Member of Technical Staff (Forward Deployed Engineer, Applied AI)
fitScoreNote: I've spent the last two years as a forward-deployed engineer embedded with enterprise healthcare customers, building production RAG systems with pgvector and OpenAI embeddings, shipping a five-agent parallel pipeline on Claude Code, and closing $31.8M in pipeline by being the person who could both architect the solution and explain it to a CIO. My explicit 'forward-deployed delivery engineer' title at Thoughtful AI, 57 production Python integration scripts across Notion, Fireflies, Google Drive, Slack, and GitHub, and a hybrid-RAG system hitting 95% accuracy on live patient data map directly to what Perplexity is asking for. The honest gap is that my background comes from the customer-facing and analytics side rather than pure software engineering, and my distributed systems and JavaScript depth are growth areas rather than proven strengths.

coreJobFunction.rationale: Dan has strong evidence of bridging technical and non-technical stakeholders (particularly at Thoughtful AI and Google), customer-facing delivery and GTM motions, and some direct building/prototyping. However, the Perplexity FDE role requires production-grade LLM system deployment, RAG/retrieval architecture, agent workflow engineering, and deep Python/API integration work at an engineering-execution level that goes well beyond solutions engineering. The healthcare-agent-data-layer project suggests some agentic AI exposure, but there is insufficient evidence of the core technical depth this role demands — prompt engineering in production, vector/embedding systems, distributed systems debugging, and shipping LLM-powered integrations at enterprise scale.

seniority.rationale: Dan has ~8 years of relevant experience and demonstrates genuine, production-grade LLM/agentic AI work: he built a five-agent parallel meeting intelligence pipeline, 57 Python API integration scripts across Notion/Fireflies/Google/Slack/GitHub, event-driven agent hooks, CI/CD workflows, and a Claude Code-based SE platform managing $31.8M in enterprise healthcare pipeline — all end-to-end and owned solo. This maps well to the forward deployed engineer profile. The gap is that his background is analytics and solutions engineering rather than software engineering, his distributed systems and low-latency/infrastructure depth is limited, and he has no explicit RAG, vector database, or search systems experience. He meets the 5+ year threshold and ownership bar, but the pure SWE and production AI infrastructure depth the role expects (prompt evaluation frameworks, scalable LLM deployment, distributed system debugging) is partially present rather than fully demonstrated.

technicalSkills.rationale: Dan has strong evidence of production-level LLM and agentic AI work: he built a hybrid-RAG system using OpenAI embeddings, pgvector, and GPT-3.5/4o achieving 95% accuracy in production, a 5-agent parallel meeting intelligence pipeline using Claude Code with event-driven hooks, and 57 Python integration scripts across Notion, Fireflies, Google Drive, GitHub, and Slack APIs. His Python depth is solid and demonstrated across a decade of production use (SQLAlchemy, Pandas, Flask, Airflow, AWS), and he has direct forward-deployed engineering experience (explicitly titled 'forward-deployed delivery engineer' at Thoughtful, leading FDEs at Lead TPM level). Key gaps relative to this specific role: no evidence of JavaScript/TypeScript as a secondary language, no explicit experience with distributed systems authentication patterns or latency optimization at scale, and his RAG/embedding work is narrowly scoped to healthcare RCM rather than general search/retrieval infrastructure — which is Perplexity's core domain.

industryVertical.rationale: Perplexity's ICP is enterprise teams (healthcare, finance, and other regulated industries are explicitly called out as nice-to-haves) buying AI search, retrieval, and workflow automation — and Dan has spent 2+ years embedded in exactly those enterprise buyers as a forward-deployed Customer Engineer and Director of SE at healthcare AI companies. The role is structurally vague/horizontal (enterprise AI infrastructure), but the buyer persona — enterprise IT, CIOs, revenue operations leaders — maps closely to Dan's primary customer base. The healthcare vertical specifically is named in the JD as a preferred background, and Dan's depth there is substantial. The slight deduction from a 9-10 is that Perplexity also targets non-healthcare enterprise buyers (developer tools, general enterprise SaaS, finance) where Dan has less direct ICP exposure.

logistics.rationale: The role is explicitly listed as New York City / San Francisco, and Dan has been based in San Francisco since January 2020 with deep personal roots there (partner, home, upcoming 2025 wedding), eliminating any location friction. No compensation band is stated in the JD, but Perplexity MTS roles at this seniority align with the $200K–$300K+ SF senior IC market range assumed open. There are no stated requirements for security clearances, specific degrees, language certifications, or mandatory relocation — and the baseline facts confirm Dan is travel-tolerant for enterprise customer-facing work, which fits the forward-deployed nature of this role.

summary: Built a production hybrid-RAG system using OpenAI embeddings, pgvector on AWS RDS, and GPT-3.5/4o that achieved 95% accuracy on live patient eligibility data within two days of deployment, with a daily ingestion pipeline pulling human-verified examples.

Wrote 57 Python integration scripts across Notion, Fireflies, Google Drive, Slack, and GitHub APIs, all production-owned solo, as part of a Claude Code platform managing $31.8M in active enterprise healthcare pipeline.

Designed and shipped a five-agent parallel meeting intelligence pipeline with event-driven hooks, pre/post tool-use logging, and a user-signal-driven feedback loop, mapping directly to the agentic workflow architecture Perplexity deploys in production.

Held explicit 'forward-deployed delivery engineer' title at Thoughtful AI and subsequently led a team of FDEs as Lead TPM, running technical discovery, integration scoping, and production rollouts embedded with enterprise healthcare customers across 60+ deals.

Retained as one of only a handful of non-Product/Engineering employees when Smarter Technologies reduced headcount by 85%, and promoted to VP of Solutions at AccessHealthcare within a month, on the strength of bridging complex AI system architecture and enterprise buyer communication.
jobDescriptionText:
Member of Technical Staff (Forward Deployed Engineer, Applied AI)
Company: Perplexity
Location: New York City / San Francisco
Department: Engineering

INTRODUCTION
Perplexity is building AI systems that integrate directly into how enterprises operate. Our API Platform powers search, retrieval, and automation across structured and unstructured data, while Perplexity Computer extends this into the execution of an AI system that navigates tools, interacts with applications, and completes multi-step workflows. Together, these platforms form a new integration layer, connecting models, data, and enterprise systems into end-to-end workflows.

ABOUT THE ROLE
We're looking for Forward Deployed Engineers to work directly with customers to design and deploy these integrations in production. You'll embed with teams, connect Perplexity into their existing stack, and build systems that automate real work.

This role spans two closely connected areas:
- API Platform: integrating search, retrieval, and AI capabilities into enterprise systems
- Perplexity Computer: deploying agentic workflows that operate across tools, applications, and data

RESPONSIBILITIES
- Design, build, and deploy end-to-end integrations between Perplexity and enterprise systems (data platforms, internal tools, SaaS applications), translating business workflows into production-grade AI systems
- Work directly with customer teams to embed AI into existing processes, owning deployments from initial architecture through production rollout and ongoing optimization
- Develop and operationalize integrations using APIs, event-driven architectures, and workflow orchestration, including deploying Perplexity Computer for multi-step, agent-driven workflows across tools and environments
- Design and build production systems that combine retrieval, reasoning, and execution across enterprise environments, applying deep expertise in LLM capabilities, implementation patterns, and the AI stack to drive performance, security, and customer impact
- Debug and resolve issues across APIs, infrastructure, and external dependencies, ensuring reliability, performance, and scalability in production
- Prototype new integration patterns and build reusable architectures that accelerate adoption across customers
- Partner with Sales and Product to unlock new use cases, drive expansion, and translate deployment learnings into product and platform improvements

WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR
- 5+ years of experience in software engineering, forward deployed engineering, solutions engineering, or similar roles, with a track record of building and shipping production systems in customer-facing environments
- Strong programming ability in Python (plus one of JavaScript/TypeScript, Java, etc.) with experience developing integrations, prototypes, and scalable applications
- Deep experience with APIs and distributed systems, including authentication, latency optimization, and debugging across complex, multi-system environments
- Production experience building LLM-powered systems, including prompt engineering, agent workflows, evaluation, and deploying AI systems at scale
- Proven ability to design and implement automated, end-to-end workflows that integrate across enterprise systems and replace manual processes
- High ownership and ability to operate in ambiguous environments, with strong system design, rapid prototyping skills, and end-to-end execution
- Excellent communication and collaboration skills, with experience working cross-functionally and engaging both technical teams and executive stakeholders

NICE-TO-HAVES
- Experience with search systems, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), or AI/ML APIs
- Background in developer tools, platform engineering, or high-scale/low-latency system design
- History of working at startups or small teams, owning customer projects end-to-end
- Experience with enterprise IT systems or AI deployment patterns in regulated industries (finance, healthcare, life sciences)

strengths.title: Production RAG and Agentic AI Systems Built and Owned
strengths.description: I built a hybrid-RAG system in production, not in a sandbox, using OpenAI embeddings, pgvector on AWS RDS, and a daily import pipeline of human-verified examples that drove the model toward convergent determinism as real data accumulated. It hit 95% accuracy within two days on live patient eligibility data that customer agents used to make actual benefit determination calls. Alongside that, I designed a five-agent parallel pipeline on Claude Code with event-driven pre/post tool-use hooks and a feedback loop tied to real user signal, which is the same agentic retrieval architecture pattern this role requires.

strengths.title: Lived the Forward-Deployed Engineer Role Explicitly
strengths.description: I was literally titled a 'forward-deployed delivery engineer' at Thoughtful AI, and I later led a team of FDEs as Lead TPM. I know what the role demands at the day-to-day level: scoping architecture in the morning with a customer IT team, debugging a failing OAuth2 integration in the afternoon, and presenting precision/recall metrics to a CIO that evening. I've run that motion embedded at enterprise healthcare customers across 60+ deals, which means I understand the customer trust dynamics, the security questions about data residency and BAAs, and the gap between a working demo and a production rollout.

strengths.title: 57 Production Python API Integrations, Solo-Owned
strengths.description: I wrote 57 Python integration scripts across Notion, Fireflies, Google Drive, Slack, and GitHub APIs, all production, all owned end-to-end without a team, as part of a system that manages $31.8M in active enterprise pipeline. These are not utility scripts: they include CI/CD workflows, event-driven agent hooks, and OAuth2 authentication patterns across multiple external dependencies. My Python depth runs across a decade of production use including SQLAlchemy, Pandas, Flask, Airflow, and AWS, which covers the core integration surface this role operates on.

strengths.title: Enterprise Healthcare Buyer Persona: Deep Embedded Experience
strengths.description: Perplexity's JD calls out healthcare as a preferred vertical, and I've spent two years embedded in exactly that buyer: healthcare CIOs, IT security teams, revenue cycle directors at health systems and payer organizations. I know how they evaluate AI adoption risk, what questions they ask about BAAs and data residency, and what it takes to move them from skeptical to signed. That context, combined with 60+ enterprise deals in my pipeline history, means I can run technical discovery, translate architecture into buyer language, and accelerate adoption in the vertical Perplexity is prioritizing.

strengths.title: Eval Infrastructure Built with Real Financial Consequences
strengths.description: I built and operated LLM eval infrastructure with holdout datasets, precision/recall tracking by category, and regression tests because accuracy had direct financial consequences for the customers I was serving: incorrect benefit determinations meant real claim denials and revenue loss. That discipline, building evals before trusting a system in production, is something I'd bring directly to Perplexity's integration work where enterprise customers need reliability guarantees, not just demo-quality outputs. My eval framework for the dental eligibility system tracked improvement as new verified examples accumulated, enabling a data-driven accuracy roadmap.

weaknesses.title: No Production JavaScript or TypeScript Experience
weaknesses.description: My secondary language exposure beyond Python has been through integration work: interfacing with Next.js teams and building against their APIs, but not owning JavaScript or TypeScript codebases in production myself.
weaknesses.mitigation: I would be direct about this gap in interviews and commit to closing it in the first 30 days. My Computer Engineering foundation means I pick up new languages at the syntax level quickly; the harder part is idiomatic production patterns, and I would prioritize pairing with Perplexity engineers on any TypeScript integration work while I ramp. I have built full-stack systems in Python and interfaced with JS-heavy stacks, so the conceptual surface is not new.

weaknesses.title: Distributed Systems Depth is Integration-Level, Not Infrastructure-Level
weaknesses.description: My distributed systems experience covers production API integration across OAuth2, event-driven agent architectures, and debugging across multi-step tool-call chains in Claude Code, but I have not built distributed systems from scratch or owned production infrastructure at cloud scale with low-latency optimization requirements.
weaknesses.mitigation: I would frame what I do have clearly and not overstate it: I have debugged across complex multi-system API environments, operated CI/CD pipelines, and built on AWS RDS with pgvector in production. I would ask the interviewer specifically what 'distributed systems depth' means for the forward-deployed scope of this role versus the core platform engineering scope, and answer to that. Scaling those integration patterns to Perplexity's infrastructure level is a growth area I am genuinely excited about, not one I would pretend away.

weaknesses.title: RAG Experience is Healthcare-Scoped, Not General Search Infrastructure
weaknesses.description: My hybrid-RAG system was built for dental eligibility and healthcare RCM specifically, which is a narrow domain relative to Perplexity's general enterprise search and retrieval infrastructure that spans developer tools, finance, and general SaaS.
weaknesses.mitigation: I would lean into the architectural depth rather than the domain breadth: I built the embedding pipeline, designed the pgvector schema, wrote the daily ingestion and eval framework, and tracked accuracy improvement over time. The architecture is transferable; the domain is different. I have transferred technical skillsets across verticals before, and the Healthcare Agent Data Layer project I have been developing independently shows I can reason at a systems level about retrieval architecture beyond any single use case. I would be honest that general enterprise search infrastructure is new territory and frame it as exactly the kind of expanded scope I am coming to Perplexity to work on.

referralBlurb:
Dan Mathieson is one of the cleaner fits I have seen for a forward-deployed engineering role at a company like Perplexity, and I say that with a specific qualification in mind: his differentiation is not that he is the deepest infrastructure engineer you will interview. It is that he is the person who built a production hybrid-RAG system, owned it solo, hit 95% accuracy on live patient eligibility data within two days, and then walked into a CIO's office and explained why it worked. That combination is rare and hard to hire for.

His technical execution is real. He wrote 57 Python integration scripts across Notion, Fireflies, Google Drive, Slack, and GitHub APIs, all production, all solo-owned, as part of a Claude Code platform managing $31.8M in active enterprise pipeline. He designed a five-agent parallel meeting intelligence pipeline with event-driven hooks and a user-signal feedback loop. He built eval infrastructure with holdout datasets and precision/recall tracking by category because his customers' revenue depended on accuracy. He was explicitly titled a forward-deployed delivery engineer at Thoughtful AI and later led a team of FDEs. He has lived the motion this role requires.

His customer-side depth is equally strong. He has run technical discovery with healthcare CIOs, IT security teams, and revenue cycle directors across 60-plus enterprise deals. He knows what those buyers ask about data residency, BAAs, and AI adoption risk. The Perplexity JD calls out healthcare as a preferred vertical, and Dan has spent two years embedded in exactly that buyer persona.

The honest gaps are JavaScript/TypeScript experience and distributed systems depth at infrastructure scale. He is transparent about both and has a credible path to closing them given his Computer Engineering foundation and demonstrated ability to ramp quickly.

I would recommend getting Dan in front of your engineering team. He is not a traditional SWE, and he would tell you that directly. But for a forward-deployed role where the job is to embed with enterprise customers, ship production integrations, and translate complex AI systems into business outcomes, he has the evidence to back it up.

projects.name: Dental Eligibility Intelligence
projects.relevance: This production hybrid-RAG system using OpenAI embeddings, pgvector, and GPT-3.5/4o, achieving 95% accuracy on live patient data with a daily ingestion pipeline and eval framework, demonstrates exactly the kind of retrieval-augmented, production-grade AI integration Perplexity's FDE role requires.

projects.name: AI Meeting Intelligence
projects.relevance: This five-agent parallel pipeline on Claude Code with event-driven hooks, pre/post tool-use logging, and automated feedback loops maps directly to the agentic workflow architecture Perplexity deploys through Perplexity Computer for multi-step, tool-driven enterprise workflows.

projects.name: Personal Website Agent
projects.relevance: This full-stack AI agent built on the Anthropic SDK with tool use, streaming, RAG pipeline, and multiple LLM backends demonstrates Dan's ability to design and ship end-to-end LLM-powered systems independently, across the full integration stack this role operates on.
